summary: No-ship: the orchestrated create path does not persist the orchestrator/gateway session id at launch, and it treats any returned `promptPath` as valid without checking that the file exists/readable before spawning `claude`.

findings.title: Orchestrated session creation never stores the returned session id, so exact resume semantics are lost
findings.recommendation: Extend the placement response/contract to include the session id, persist it onto the session record before or during create, and cover that path with a test. As written, only `promptPath` and `model` are consumed, so a just-created orchestrated tab still has `claudeSessionId: null` until some later hook updates state.

findings.title: A successful placement response with a bad or unreadable prompt path does not trigger fallback; it launches `claude` with a broken `--append-system-prompt-file` instead
findings.recommendation: Validate that `spec.promptPath` exists and is readable before threading it into `ensurePty`; otherwise clear `orchestrated` and fall back to the bare session. Add a regression test for the placement-fails / missing-prompt fallback path.
